title: Thomas Cup Squad Reassembles for Asian Games, Treesa-Gayatri Return
indian_angle: This news confirms the Indian badminton contingent for the upcoming Asian Games, highlighting key players like Treesa and Gayatri.
summary: India's Thomas Cup squad members are reassembling for the Asian Games, with Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand returning to the team. The article mentions PV Sindhu, Lakshya Sen, Unnati Hooda, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy, and Chirag Shetty as part of the contingent.

title: Tanvi Sharma's Game Compared to Saina Nehwal and PV Sindhu
indian_angle: Indian fans are keen to see how young talent Tanvi Sharma's playing style evolves, with comparisons drawn to legendary Indian shuttlers Saina Nehwal and PV Sindhu.
summary: An analysis delves into the playing style of young Indian shuttler Tanvi Sharma, drawing parallels between her game development and that of former world champions Saina Nehwal and PV Sindhu.

title: Grassroot Coaches Development Program Launched in Guwahati and Raipur
indian_angle: This program is crucial for nurturing future badminton talent across India by enhancing coaching standards at the grassroots level in key regions.
summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has initiated a Coaches Development Program at the grassroots level, with sessions running from June 12-21 in National Centres of Excellence (NCE) in Guwahati and Raipur.

title: India Invites EOI for BWF World Championships 2026 Organization
indian_angle: India's expression of interest to host the BWF World Championships 2026 signifies a major step for the country's presence on the international badminton stage.
summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has issued an Expression of Interest for the organization of the BWF World Championships 2026, indicating India's potential to host the prestigious international tournament.

title: Satwik-Chirag Crowned Singapore Open Champions After Thrilling Final
indian_angle: This victory marks another significant milestone for India's top men's doubles pair, solidifying their position among the world's elite.
summary: India's star men's doubles pair, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty, clinched the Singapore Open title after a display of furious rallies and powerful smashes, followed by their signature celebratory dances.

title: New 3x15 Scoring System for Junior Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: This rule change directly impacts young Indian shuttlers, potentially influencing game strategy and development in junior categories nationwide.
summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has announced the implementation of a new 3x15 scoring system for domestic ranking tournaments and National Championships in the U-13, U-15, and U-17 age categories.
